perturbation.original_text: Upon the signing of this Agreement, Client agrees to pay to Company a total of $5,000. Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 on completion of the Scope of Work.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the signing of this Agreement, Client agrees to pay to Company a total of $5,000. Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 within 15 days of signing this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment terms. Originally, the remaining $3,100 was due 'on completion of the Scope of Work.' The modified text changes this to 'within 15 days of signing this agreement.' This contradicts the original intent as the completion of work may take longer than 15 days, leading to uncertainty and potential dispute on the payment schedule. It also contradicts the clause stating the project will take 12 weeks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Terms of the project: 12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms, originally tied to the 'completion of the Scope of Work,' to 'within 15 days of signing this agreement.' This directly contradicts the 'Terms of the project' which states the project will take '12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018.' The client is now obligated to pay $3,100 within 15 days, regardless of whether the work is completed, creating a clear inconsistency in payment schedule as the work spans 12 weeks.

perturbation.original_text: Company will use its best efforts to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Terms of the project: 12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: Company guarantees to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Terms of the project: 12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are not subject to delay under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the delivery terms of the Client Website. Initially, Company uses 'best efforts' to deliver in the specified timeframe, and delays were permitted under certain client-related issues. Now, the company 'guarantees' delivery, and delays are not allowed. This creates contradiction because unforeseen circumstances like client delays are inevitable, which creates uncertainty over contractual obligations and raises concerns in the event that a delay is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company will use its best efforts to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Terms of the project: 12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text allows for delays under certain circumstances, stating, "Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client." The changed text explicitly states that "any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are not subject to delay under any circumstances." This directly contradicts the initial clause, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as unforeseen client-related issues were initially considered acceptable reasons for delay but are now explicitly prohibited.

perturbation.original_text: Any content submitted by Client in any other manner or format, including, but not limited to Adobe PDF format, will be returned to Client for resubmission. Company will not make any attempt to proof read or correct any contextual, grammatical or typographical errors in the written content submitted by Client. It is Client's sole responsibility to check the accuracy of the written content and correct any errors prior to submission for final publication. Company will assume that all the written content submitted by Client has been proofread and is ready for publication. Client may elect to pay Company the Hourly Rate set forth below to type and proofread any written content not submitted in the electronic formats specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: Any content submitted by Client in any manner or format will be accepted. Company will proof read and correct any contextual, grammatical or typographical errors in the written content submitted by Client. It is Company's sole responsibility to check the accuracy of the written content and correct any errors prior to submission for final publication. Company will assume that all the written content submitted by Client has been proofread and is ready for publication. Client may elect to pay Company the Hourly Rate set forth below to type and proofread any written content not submitted in the electronic formats specified above.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by altering who is responsible for proofreading the Client's submitted content. Initially, the client is responsible and non-compliant submissions are rejected. With this perturbation, the Company becomes responsible for proofreading and all formats are accepted. While the Company assumes that content has been proofread, they also take responsibility to do so as well which is logically inconsistent, because the company assumes that Client has already proofread and at the same time, in the lines above it, the text states that the Company will proofread. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes over the quality of the final product, especially if errors persist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any content submitted by Client in any other manner or format, including, but not limited to Adobe PDF format, will be returned to Client for resubmission. Company will not make any attempt to proof read or correct any contextual, grammatical or typographical errors in the written content submitted by Client. It is Client's sole responsibility to check the accuracy of the written content and correct any errors prior to submission for final publication. Company will assume that all the written content submitted by Client has been proofread and is ready for publication. Client may elect to pay Company the Hourly Rate set forth below to type and proofread any written content not submitted in the electronic formats specified above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by shifting the responsibility for proofreading from the Client to the Company. The original text and contradicted text explicitly state that the Company will not proofread and that it is the Client's sole responsibility. The changed text states the Company will proofread while also claiming the Client is still responsible for proofreading, which creates an internal inconsistency as to who is ultimately responsible and if content should be rejected, because it also states that "Any content submitted by Client in any manner or format will be accepted." This directly contradicts the repeated assertion that the client is responsible, leading to confusion and potential disputes.
